items.headline: Historic home at Crescent Avenue to be redeveloped with four townhomes
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission considered a proposal to modify a historic resource at 434 Crescent Avenue by retaining the original 960-square foot house while adding four new three-story townhomes in the rear. The project requires approval of a Resource Alteration Permit, which involves balancing preservation of the historic structure with new residential development.

items.headline: City Council approves new digital signage agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This could affect where and how digital ads appear throughout San José.

items.headline: City seeks proposals for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The council issued a report on requests for proposals to contract cleanup services for encampments, indicating a new or expanded approach to managing homeless encampments in the city.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward
items.summary: The council took actions related to replacing an elevator at the Terminal, likely a significant infrastructure upgrade affecting downtown San José's transportation hub.

items.headline: City negotiating property purchase with school district at Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District over buying or acquiring property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The details of what the city plans to do with this land are not yet public.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, which will guide development of green space and recreational infrastructure in this key area. This plan shapes what parks and trails residents will have access to as the Innovation District develops over the coming years.

items.headline: Major 260-unit apartment complex coming to Berryessa
items.summary: Berryessa Family Apartments, a $52.4M new construction project, will add 260 units of housing to the Berryessa area. This is a significant housing development that should help address local supply constraints.

items.headline: Arc'Teryx flagship retail opening at Stevens Creek
items.summary: Outdoor apparel brand Arc'Teryx is opening a $1.7M retail location at Stevens Creek Boulevard. The substantial investment signals confidence in San José's retail market and brings a premium outdoor retail destination to the area.
